deploy/charts.purpose: Organisational parent for the two Helm charts (benthos-collector, openmeter) that package all OpenMeter Kubernetes workloads. Owns the shared helm-docs generation pipeline (Makefile + template.md) consumed by both child charts.

patterns.name: helm-docs via shared template
patterns.description: READMEs for both child charts are generated by `helm-docs` using the shared template.md defined at this level. Run `make docs` to regenerate. Never hand-edit a chart README.md.
patterns.example: make docs  # invokes helm-docs with template.md for benthos-collector and openmeter

patterns.name: OCI Helm registry distribution
patterns.description: Charts are packaged with `helm package deploy/charts/<CHART> --version <VERSION> --destination build/helm` and pushed to ghcr.io/openmeterio/helm-charts/. Chart.yaml version and appVersion must stay aligned with the container image tag.

patterns.name: Self-contained child charts
patterns.description: Each child chart (benthos-collector, openmeter) must be fully self-contained. No shared Kubernetes manifests, CRDs, or cluster-scoped RBAC live at the deploy/charts level.
patterns.example: # Each chart has its own templates/, values.yaml, Chart.yaml, and README.tmpl.md

key_file_guides.role: Runs helm-docs across both child charts using the shared template.md. The sole command is `make docs`.
key_file_guides.watch_for: helm-docs must be available (it is in the Nix CI shell). Running outside Nix silently produces no output if helm-docs is missing.

key_file_guides.role: Shared Go-template for helm-docs defining badge helpers (versionBadge, typeBadge, appVersionBadge, artifactHubBadge) and the tldr / chart.base layout consumed by both child README templates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Editing badge or section templates here affects both child chart READMEs. The artifactHubBadge URL is derived from `.Name`, so chart names must not change without updating ArtifactHub listings.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all named template helpers for the benthos-collector chart: fullname, componentName, labels, selectorLabels, benthos-collector.args.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All resource names and args must route through these helpers — never hardcode release or chart name strings directly in resource files.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all named template helpers for the openmeter chart: componentName, fullname, componentLabels, componentSelectorLabels.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All app.kubernetes.io/* labels and resource names must be generated through these helpers — inline label sets will diverge from selector labels causing broken Services.

key_file_guides.role: Single shared ConfigMap mounted as /etc/openmeter/config.yaml across all OpenMeter binaries. Optional infrastructure blocks are guarded by enabled flags.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every optional infrastructure section (Kafka, ClickHouse, Redis, Svix) must have a matching `{{- if .Values.<service>.enabled -}}` guard here AND in the corresponding resource template.

key_file_guides.role: Single source of defaults for all OpenMeter workloads. All OpenMeter binaries share Values.image.repository and Values.image.tag — only Svix uses its own image fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add per-binary image fields — version skew across mono-repo binaries is prevented by sharing a single image tag.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing a child chart README.md — it is generated by helm-docs from template.md and values.yaml comments; edits will be overwritten on next `make docs`
Adding a third chart at this level without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and updating the Makefile docs target
Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at deploy/charts level — each chart must be fully self-contained
Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag used in values.yaml
Adding optional infrastructure to the openmeter chart without both an enabled guard in the resource template AND a corresponding override block in configmap.yaml

decisions.decision: Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart
decisions.rationale: Both charts share the same badge set and TL;DR install pattern; centralising the template ensures consistent branding and eliminates duplication across helm-docs runs.

decisions.decision: OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repository index
decisions.rationale: OCI registries are supported natively by Helm 3+ and integrate with the same GHCR credentials used for container images, simplifying the release pipeline.

decisions.decision: All OpenMeter binaries share one image and tag in the openmeter chart
decisions.rationale: The mono-repo produces all binaries from a single Dockerfile and release tag; sharing one image tag enforces version consistency and prevents accidental partial upgrades.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new OpenMeter workload to the openmeter chart
